Frank Joseph Boyd was formally charged with five serious offenses following the June 28th, 2022 traffic stop.

His charges included third-degree drug possession for having more than 10 grams of methamphetamine, first-degree DWI test refusal, first-degree DWI with a prior felony conviction, violating a restricted driver's license related to alcohol use, and driving after revocation.
After Boyd was taken into custody, a search warrant was obtained to collect a urine or blood sample.

Despite repeated warnings from officers that by not completing the test, he would be adding on to his lengthy list of charges, Boyd again refused. The complaints outlined the seriousness of the offenses, a significant quantity of methamphetamine found in his vehicle, open containers of alcohol, drug paraphernalia, and the fact that Boyd had nine prior DWI convictions, including a first-degree felony DWI in 2011. His driving privileges were already revoked with alcohol restrictions, yet he continued to drive under the influence. On September 11th, 2023, Frank Joseph Boyd was convicted of felony third-degree drug possession, felony DWI refusal, and misdemeanor driving after revocation. He was sentenced to nearly four years in prison, with all sentences running concurrently, the longest being 51 months. He received credit for 133 days already served.

As part of Minnesota's challenge incarceration program, Boyd was eligible for early release under intense supervision and got out of prison in late 2024. For exclusive clips and behind-the-scenes content, join us on patreon.com/midwestcrime.
